query:
Can Difference-in-Difference be used when the treatment effects get smaller with time since treatment?

Recently, there is an emerging line of the study said that the traditional two-way fixed effect(TWFE) is failed in a lot of case because of the heterogeneous effects of laws over time, follow some paper Goodman-Bacon (2019), Chaisemartin, 2020.

Especially, Goodman, 2019 did a great job to decompose the single post-treatment dummy. In his note, he answered the question "Is DD wrong":

Not in general. The DD research design—comparing outcomes for groups whose treatment status changes to groups whose treatment status does not change—still can be a good idea. The DD specification—estimating the coefficient a single post-treatment dummy—is a bad idea when your treatment effects vary over time (get bigger with time since treatment). In this case, just summarize your findings in a different way—event-study or a linear trend-break, for instance.

My question here is, if I expect and argue that the treatment effects get smaller with time since treatment, so whether DD specification now is a bad idea?
llm_reasoning: The query involves understanding the applicability of a statistical method (Difference-in-Differences) under specific conditions (treatment effects getting smaller over time). It also references existing literature and asks for an evaluation based on that context. The reasoning is sequential, as it builds on the understanding of the method and its limitations, and then applies this to a specific scenario.

query:
KPIs re-indexing after some years

I just realized that eurostat CPI index has three values of reference:

1996, 2005, 2015

https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/databrowser/view/PRC_HICP_MANR__custom_120601/bookmark/table?lang=en&bookmarkId=952bcf60-22e8-433b-ab93-fe85e2ab2367

https://ec.europa.eu/eurostat/cache/metadata/en/prc_hicp_esms.htm#ref_period1682076048145
Being myself an engineer, not that familiar with economy KPIs, I wonder:

What need does this re-index tries to solve, or why is it performed?

Thank you so much!!
llm_reasoning: The query is asking for an explanation of why re-indexing of KPIs (in this case, the CPI index) is performed. This is a single, open-ended question that does not have a clear logical structure or multiple independent sub-queries. It is also not conditional or a mix of different types. The question is seeking a general understanding and explanation, which is more subjective and lacks a clear, structured reasoning path.
